name: PageRank
applicable_scenarios: rank nodes in a directed graph by stationary importance
influence ranking in social / citation / web graphs
baseline centrality measure for directed networks
python_template:
import numpy as np

def pagerank(A, alpha=0.85, tol=1e-8, max_iter=200):
    # A: adjacency matrix, rows = outlinks. Dangling rows handled via teleport.
    A = np.asarray(A, dtype=float)
    n = A.shape[0]
    out = A.sum(axis=1, keepdims=True)
    out[out == 0] = 1.0
    M = (A / out).T
    r = np.ones(n) / n
    teleport = (1 - alpha) / n
    for _ in range(max_iter):
        rn = alpha * (M @ r) + teleport
        if np.linalg.norm(rn - r, 1) < tol:
            return rn
        r = rn
    return r
typical_cases: ranking academic papers in a citation network
importance of users in a follower / retweet graph
relevance scoring in a web-link graph
common_pitfalls: dangling nodes (no outlinks) need teleport handling or they sink probability
damping factor alpha strongly affects results; 0.85 is conventional
not informative on small / dense graphs — degree centrality often suffices
power iteration may be slow on poorly-conditioned graphs
